free_text: Excitation Functions For the Cyclotron Production of
99-M-Tc and 99-Mo.

Zn-62

the data were averaged and were used up to 45.5 MeV.
Ni-57 and
Co-58
The data were used above 45.5 MeV
The Measurements were Repeated Several Times
To Follow Decay of the Radioactive Products and Thereby
To Identify the Possible Interferring Nuckides.
Two Julich Cyclotrons were Used:
1. Compact Cyclotron Cv-28,
2. Injector of Cooler Synchrotron.
Cyclone.
A Total of Five Stacks, Each Consisting of Four
To Six Samples of Mo-100 and About the Same Number of
Trioxide of Mo-98, As Well As Several Copper Monitor
Foils and Some Copper and Aluminium Foils As Energy
Degraders.
At the Two Cyclotrons in Julich the Beam
Current was Measured Also Via a Faraday Cup Within an
Uncertainty of About 10%. The Twoindependent
Measurements Agreed Within the Limits of Errors.
Last checking has been done.
DATE is corrected
The Total Uncertainty in the Proton Beam
Current was Estimated to be 5-12%. The Uncertainty in
The Peak Area Analysis was < 5%, Detector Efficiency
Uncertainty was Estimated to be <5%.  The Uncertainty
Of Target Thickness was <2%.
All the Uncertainties were Combined in Quadrature.
The Uncertainty is Given in Authors' Tables.
Correction For Interfering Processes were Included.
Correction For Random Pile-Up and Coincidence Loss were
Negligible.
From Original Paper.
